channel.title: NPR Topics: National
channel.description: NPR coverage of national news, U.S. politics, elections, business, arts, culture, health and science, and technology. Subscribe to the NPR Nation RSS feed.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Immigration board denies Mahmoud Khalil's appeal
item.description: The Board of Immigration Appeals has denied Mahmoud Khalil's latest attempt to dismiss his deportation case. This decision brings the Palestinian activist one step closer to possible expulsion.
item.content:encoded:
The Board of Immigration Appeals has denied Mahmoud Khalil's latest attempt to dismiss his deportation case. This decision brings the Palestinian activist one step closer to possible expulsion.

(Image credit: Matt Rourke)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Melania Trump denies close ties to Jeffrey Epstein in rare public statement
item.description: The first lady made a rare public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with the late sex offender. She also said Epstein did not introduce her to President Donald Trump.
item.content:encoded:
The first lady made a rare public statement on Thursday saying she was not friends with the late sex offender. She also said Epstein did not introduce her to President Donald Trump.

(Image credit: Samuel Corum)
item.dc:creator: Ava Berger

item.title: How a cheese sandwich at the Masters is still $1.50 in an era of price hikes
item.description: The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.
item.content:encoded:
The priciest concessions at the Masters, beer and wine, cost just $6 each. The Georgia golf tournament prides itself on a simple and affordable menu, even as ticket prices continue to climb.

(Image credit: Andrew Redington)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Israel vows to continue fighting Hezbollah but agrees to talks with Lebanon
item.description: Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.
item.content:encoded:
Israel's prime minister said his government would begin talks with Lebanon but vowed to continue attacks against the Iran-backed Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, despite a fragile Iran ceasefire.

(Image credit: Fadel Itani)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: What's behind Trump's seemingly lackluster response to Artemis II's lunar mission
item.description: Many people have been enchanted watching Artemis II circle the moon and start the journey home. President Trump doesn't appear to be among them. Some critics say his lack of enthusiasm is calculated.
item.content:encoded: Many people have been enchanted watching Artemis II circle the moon and start the journey home. President Trump doesn't appear to be among them. Some critics say his lack of enthusiasm is calculated.
item.dc:creator: Katia Riddle

item.title: In Vancouver, Squamish Nation builds what many cities struggle to: lots of housing
item.description: In Vancouver, the Squamish Nation had a chance to do what many housing advocates dream of, developing a plot of prime urban real estate without zoning constraints. So what did they build? Housing.
item.content:encoded: In Vancouver, the Squamish Nation had a chance to do what many housing advocates dream of, developing a plot of prime urban real estate without zoning constraints. So what did they build? Housing.
item.dc:creator: Alex Mayyasi

item.title: As gaming's popularity rises, here's how parents can talk to kids about gambling
item.description: Teens and young adults have a growing problem with sports betting and gaming. How can parents talk to their kids about gambling before it becomes a problem?
item.content:encoded: Teens and young adults have a growing problem with sports betting and gaming. How can parents talk to their kids about gambling before it becomes a problem?
item.dc:creator: Sequoia Carrillo

item.title: Some of Trump's biggest supporters become his loudest critics over Iran war
item.description: The war with Iran has split many of President Trump's supporters, but rather than criticize the policy, some of the biggest names in the MAGA movement are criticizing Trump.
item.content:encoded: The war with Iran has split many of President Trump's supporters, but rather than criticize the policy, some of the biggest names in the MAGA movement are criticizing Trump.
item.dc:creator: Elena Moore

item.title: Jim Whittaker, the first American to summit Mount Everest, dies at 97
item.description: The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.
item.content:encoded:
The celebrated mountaineer, who also served as the first full-time employee of the outdoor retailer REI and later as its president and CEO, died Tuesday at his home in Port Townsend, Washington, his family said.

(Image credit: Jeff Chiu)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Guatemalan man pleads guilty in federal court in crash that killed over 50 in Mexico
item.description: A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.
item.content:encoded:
A man pleaded guilty Wednesday in federal court and acknowledged his involvement in an attempt to illegally smuggle migrants to the U.S. when a truck crashed in Mexico in 2021, killing more than 50.

(Image credit: AP)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
